personality_voice.book_baseline: Ronan is established as a centaur of deep, sorrowful voice and cryptic, evasive speech. He speaks in cosmic generalities ('Mars is bright tonight,' 'Always the innocent are the first victims,' 'The forest hides many secrets'), deflects direct questions, and stares skyward rather than engaging with immediate concerns. He is gloomy, cautious, and non-committal — notably more moderate than Bane, as shown when he nervously defends Firenze ('I'm sure Firenze thought he was acting for the best') while still not openly siding with him. In the Order of the Phoenix book scenes, a grey centaur (likely Ronan or Magorian) is aggressive and contemptuous toward humans. In the Deathly Hallows battle, Ronan appears briefly as part of the centaur charge.
personality_voice.film_portrayal: The film corpus provided is empty. No film scenes featuring Ronan are included.
personality_voice.difference: No film material exists in the corpus to compare against the book baseline.
personality_voice.penalty_logic: With zero film evidence, no score can be assigned. The score is 0 by default due to complete absence of evaluable film material, not as a faithfulness judgment.

narrative_role_agency.book_baseline: Ronan functions as an introduction to centaur culture — their evasiveness, cosmic fatalism, and refusal to interfere in human affairs. He is present at the confrontation with Firenze, where he nervously mediates between Bane's anger and Firenze's defiance. He appears in the Battle of Hogwarts as part of the centaur charge. His agency is limited but consistent: he observes, deflects, and occasionally moderates.
narrative_role_agency.penalty_logic: No film corpus means no score can be assigned.

motivations_internal_conflict.book_baseline: Ronan's motivation is adherence to centaur doctrine: read the stars, do not interfere with human affairs, let fate unfold. His internal tension is subtle — he is less aggressive than Bane but does not openly break with centaur tradition as Firenze does. He is caught between instinctive caution and the centaur code.
motivations_internal_conflict.penalty_logic: No film corpus means no score can be assigned.

character_arc.book_baseline: Ronan has a flat arc across the books. He begins as a cautious, evasive centaur and ends as one who joins the Battle of Hogwarts — a notable shift for a character defined by non-interference, though this is not developed in detail. The arc is minimal and largely collective (the centaurs as a group decide to fight).
character_arc.penalty_logic: No film corpus means no score can be assigned.

key_relationships.book_baseline: Ronan's key relationships in the corpus are with Hagrid (respectful but evasive), Bane (aligned but more moderate), and Firenze (implicitly sympathetic but unwilling to openly support). These dynamics define his position within centaur society.
key_relationships.penalty_logic: No film corpus means no score can be assigned.

complexity_nuance_lost_material.book_baseline: Ronan's complexity lies in his position as the moderate centaur — neither as aggressive as Bane nor as willing to break ranks as Firenze. His gloomy, sorrowful voice and cryptic speech give him texture beyond simple obstruction. His nervous defense of Firenze ('I'm sure Firenze thought he was acting for the best') is the most humanizing moment in his book portrayal.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.penalty_logic: No film corpus means no score can be assigned.

comparative.key_observations: The film corpus is entirely empty. No score can be assigned to any dimension because there is no film material to evaluate against the book baseline. All dimension scores are 0 and all confidence levels are Low, reflecting the absence of evaluable evidence rather than a judgment of unfaithfulness. A meaningful FP score for Ronan would require at least the Sorcerer's Stone film scenes in which he appears.
